paper_title: Experimental Study of the Dynamic Characteristics of a New Antidrainage Subgrade Structure for High-Speed Railways in Diatomaceous Earth Areas

methods.description: This method analyzes the changes in dynamic stress on the subgrade structure based on the number of vibrations to assess the impact of train loads and the stability of the new antidrainage layer.

steps.description: Analyze the magnitude of dynamic stress changes based on the number of vibrations.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze dynamic stress changes with vibration count

steps.description: Calculate the average dynamic stress after stabilization.

methods.description: This method examines the changes in vibration acceleration with the number of vibrations to determine the stability and compaction of the subgrade bed.

steps.description: Plot the curve of vibration acceleration with the number of vibrations.
steps.searchable_summary: plot vibration acceleration vs. vibration count

steps.description: Analyze the stabilization of acceleration at different depths after cumulative loading.

methods.description: This method evaluates the cumulative settlement of the subgrade under fatigue loading to understand the deformation behavior and stability of the subgrade bed.

steps.description: Select observation points on the loading plate and reference points on the subgrade surface.
steps.searchable_summary: select observation and reference points for settlement

steps.description: Analyze the curve of accumulated deformation with the number of loadings.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze deformation curve with loading count

steps.description: Calculate the accumulated plastic deformation after a specified number of excitation cycles.
